mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate wage income and consumption, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency falls below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, upside inflation surprises tighten financial conditions expectations, increase the equity risk premium, and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 negative 5-day return frequency falls below 55 percent during triggered periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment reflect a negative joint shock to output and labor demand that erodes profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day return frequency falls below 55 percent when INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises jointly over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, a sustained rebound in housing starts signals improving construction activity and positive multiplier effects that lift cyclical earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day return frequency falls below 55 percent during periods when HOUST rises from a 12-month trough over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation above its trailing 12-month trend prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising nominal discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day return frequency falls below 55 percent when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4.0 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and accelerating payroll growth force the market to price a more aggressive monetary tightening path, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day return frequency falls below 55 percent when CPIAUCSL rises and PAYEMS rises jointly over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending power and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day return frequency falls below 55 percent following trigger activation over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production signals immediate negative earnings surprises for the manufacturing sector, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day return frequency falls below 55 percent when INDPRO declines sharply over any 2-year rolling window.
